site.name: Active Inference Institute
site.description: A participatory open science institute supporting education, research, training, and applications related to Active Inference.

pages.title: About the Institute
pages.subtitle: A public-interest institution for Active Inference education, research, and applications.
pages.lede: The Active Inference Institute is a participatory Open Science institute dedicated to improving the accessibility, rigor, and applicability of the Active Inference framework.

sections.heading: Purpose
sections.body: AII supports education, research, training, and applications related to Active Inference. The Institute welcomes all backgrounds, time zones, and familiarity levels, and organizes the ecosystem through programs, projects, public media, research resources, and governance structures.

sections.heading: Mission, Vision, Values, and Principles
sections.body: The source material emphasizes integrity, open learning, responsible conduct, professional development, excellence in research, and practical service. The public website presents these as an operating stance: make Active Inference more accessible, rigorous, and applicable while supporting ethical collaboration.

sections.heading: Institute Structure
sections.body: The Institute is represented through a Board of Directors, Officers, Scientific Advisory Board, Members, organizational units, project groups, learning groups, and public communication channels. The structure is meant to support both stable stewardship and flexible participation.

sections.heading: Information Management
sections.body: The PDF identifies Discord, Coda, newsletters, public video and podcast channels, and project pages as working information surfaces. This website refactors that information into stable public pages while preserving the source atlas for traceability.

cards.title: Open participation
cards.text: The Institute is designed for people who arrive with different time zones, skills, and familiarity levels.

cards.title: Public knowledge
cards.text: Livestreams, podcasts, readings, projects, and educational groups create reusable learning material.

cards.title: Applied rigor
cards.text: The Institute connects theoretical foundations with applied projects, tools, symposia, and ecosystem support.

pages.title: Active Inference
pages.subtitle: A framework for understanding perception, action, learning, cognition, and adaptive systems.
pages.lede: The source PDF introduces Active Inference as the central scientific and practical framework around which the Institute organizes education, research, applications, and ecosystem support.

sections.heading: What Active Inference Provides
sections.body: Active Inference connects perception, action, uncertainty, and model-based adaptation. The Institute's public material presents it as a shared framework for scientific inquiry, engineering practice, education, and collective sensemaking.

sections.heading: How to Approach the Field
sections.body: The PDF directs newcomers to the living document, the Start page, livestreams, podcasts, textbook groups, readings, and implementations. This site separates those entry points into stable public pages while keeping source-page references visible.

sections.heading: Implementations
sections.body: Implementation pathways include RxInfer.jl, PyMDP, SPM, and symbolic cognitive robotics. These tools support modeling, simulation, inference, and applied experimentation.

sections.heading: Applications
sections.body: Active Inference appears across domains including biology, neuroscience, mental health, robotics, education, economics, physics, social systems, logistics, DeSci, and scientific method.

cards.title: Framework
cards.text: A shared language for perception, action, uncertainty, and adaptive systems.

cards.title: Learning path
cards.text: Textbook groups, courses, streams, podcasts, readings, and source documents.

cards.title: Practice
cards.text: Software implementations, model-building, project work, and applied examples.

cards.title: Ecosystem
cards.text: A broad community applying the framework across scientific and practical domains.

pages.title: Activities
pages.subtitle: Recurring public meetings, project sessions, learning groups, streams, and community events.
pages.lede: Activities are the Institute's live participation surface: learning groups, model streams, project meetings, public media, and recurring updates.

sections.heading: Open Participation
sections.body: The PDF invites people to drop in to activities when it works for them. Activities are paired with public channels and project pages so participants can move between synchronous meetings and asynchronous contribution.

sections.heading: Learning Sessions
sections.body: Recurring learning activities include textbook groups, RxInfer.jl sessions, Fundamentals of Active Inference, seasonal schools, and course-oriented work.

sections.heading: Media and Production
sections.body: The public archive includes livestreams, podcasts, model streams, guest streams, symposium material, and audio-visual production work.

sections.heading: Project Sessions
sections.body: Activities connect directly to Institute Projects and Ecosystem Projects, including project meetings, preparation workflows, measurement/reporting, and presentation opportunities.

cards.title: Textbook Group
cards.text: Collaborative study of core Active Inference material.

cards.title: ModelStream
cards.text: Public sessions around modeling and applied examples.

cards.title: Project meetings
cards.text: Working sessions for Institute and ecosystem projects.

cards.title: Symposium
cards.text: Applied Active Inference presentations and sponsor pathways.

cards.title: Podcasts
cards.text: Conversational Active Inference Insights and related media.

cards.title: Weekly updates
cards.text: Recurring public orientation and progress communication.

pages.title: Programs
pages.subtitle: Structured ways to contribute, learn, partner, mentor, fund, and steward open work.
pages.lede: Institute programs turn interest into participation through volunteer, internship, mentorship, fellowship, partnership, philanthropy, grants, and open source pathways.

sections.heading: Volunteer
sections.body: Volunteering is the broadest on-ramp for participation. The source describes active and documented participation in learning groups and projects, with room for people to contribute according to available time, background, and skills.

sections.heading: Internship and Mentorship
sections.body: Internships and mentorship provide more structured engagement. These pathways help participants learn by contributing to projects, public artifacts, educational materials, research workflows, and community operations.

sections.heading: Fellows
sections.body: The Fellowship pathway supports contributors who provide regular progress reports and develop research, applications, or public resources aligned with the Institute and ecosystem.

sections.heading: Partnership and Philanthropy
sections.body: Partners and donors support shared research, public education, infrastructure, and project development. The PDF frames partnership as a practical exchange of time, attention, expertise, funding, and institutional support.

sections.heading: Open Source
sections.body: Open source is treated as an ecosystem support function: publish reusable work, improve project infrastructure, and make shared technical resources discoverable.

cards.title: Volunteer
cards.text: Contribute to learning groups, project support, operations, writing, analysis, or media.

cards.title: Internship
cards.text: Join a structured contribution pathway with learning, documentation, and project practice.

cards.title: Mentorship
cards.text: Support participant development and project continuity.

cards.title: Fellowship
cards.text: Develop research or applied work with regular reporting and public outputs.

cards.title: Partnership
cards.text: Align organizations, funders, and collaborators around concrete shared work.

cards.title: Philanthropy
cards.text: Support public education, research infrastructure, events, and ecosystem stewardship.

pages.title: Projects
pages.subtitle: Institute projects and ecosystem projects convert Active Inference into public artifacts, tools, research, education, and applied practice.
pages.lede: The PDF distinguishes Institute Projects and Ecosystem Projects, with preparation and measurement workflows for proposing, tracking, and reporting work.

sections.heading: Project Lifecycle
sections.body: Projects are prepared through a proposal process and measured through reporting updates from the Active Inference ecosystem. The public site organizes the project surface around clear titles, missions, outputs, and source-page references.

sections.heading: Institute Projects
sections.body: Institute projects include recurring research, education, production, ontology, knowledge engineering, software, symposium, and learning-group efforts. They provide internal continuity and public-facing outputs.

sections.heading: Ecosystem Projects
sections.body: Ecosystem projects are broader community initiatives, including theoretical, applied, domain-specific, and infrastructure efforts that connect Active Inference to external research and practice.

cards.title: AICACP
cards.text: A coalition-oriented project around AI alignment and care practices.

cards.title: Active Blockference
cards.text: Applies Active Inference examples and tooling in blockchain-adjacent contexts.

cards.title: Active InferAnts
cards.text: Multiagent modeling work linked to prior code and papers.

cards.title: Active Inference Journal
cards.text: A publication-oriented knowledge and research channel.

cards.title: Active Inference Ontology
cards.text: Ontology work for decentralized science and epistemic commons.

cards.title: Applied Active Inference Symposium
cards.text: A recurring symposium with prior events and a presenter/sponsor pathway.

cards.title: CogNarr
cards.text: Cognitive Narrative ecosystem work for facilitating group cognition at scale.

cards.title: Courses
cards.text: Public educational work, including social science and physics course directions.

cards.title: FarmWorks
cards.text: Applied mini-project development around soil, agriculture, and ecological modeling.

cards.title: Fundamentals of Active Inference
cards.text: A textbook and learning-group pathway for core concepts.

cards.title: Generalized Notation Notation
cards.text: A text-based notation project for communicating models.

cards.title: GEO-INFER
cards.text: Geospatial modeling and active inference techniques.

cards.title: Knowledge Engineering
cards.text: Public frontend, literature meta-analysis, and organizational knowledge infrastructure.

cards.title: RxInfer.jl Learning Group
cards.text: Learning and development around RxInfer.jl examples and applications.

cards.title: Theoretical Neurobiology
cards.text: Support for theoretical neurobiology conversations and model development.

pages.title: Learning and Research
pages.subtitle: Textbook groups, videos, podcasts, courses, research resources, implementations, and technical foundations.
pages.lede: The source PDF points learners toward the living document, livestreams, Active Inference Insights, textbook groups, readings, code implementations, and domain-specific courses.

sections.heading: How to Start
sections.body: Begin with the living document, the Start page, public livestreams, podcasts, and learning groups. The website gives a stable public map, while the Discord and recurring events provide synchronous participation.

sections.heading: Core Readings
sections.body: The PDF references the 2022 textbook by Thomas Parr, Giovanni Pezzulo, and Karl J. Friston; Maxwell Ramstead's 2023 precis; Jared Tumiel's Spinning Up in Active Inference; Beren Millidge's paper repository; and work on Active Inference ontology and teams.

sections.heading: Courses and Groups
sections.body: Learning surfaces include Fundamentals of Active Inference, Textbook Group, Social Science course, Physics course, RxInfer.jl learning group, Seasonal School, and theoretical neurobiology conversations.

sections.heading: Implementations
sections.body: The source identifies implementations including RxInfer.jl, PyMDP, SPM, and symbolic cognitive robotics. These support practical modeling and experimentation.

cards.title: Textbook Group
cards.text: A collaborative path through Active Inference foundations.

cards.title: Livestreams
cards.text: Public updates, seminars, model streams, guest streams, and educational recordings.

cards.title: Active Inference Insights
cards.text: Conversational podcast material available through public media channels.

cards.title: Research Resources
cards.text: References, repositories, papers, ontologies, and implementation links.

cards.title: Tech Tree
cards.text: A structured learning map for concepts, methods, and applications.

cards.title: Implementations
cards.text: RxInfer.jl, PyMDP, SPM, and symbolic cognitive robotics.

pages.title: Ecosystem
pages.subtitle: A broad Active Inference ecosystem spanning scientific domains, technical implementations, social systems, and applied fields.
pages.lede: The Active Inference ecosystem includes challenge areas, user segments, information architecture, organizations, projects, and domains of application.

sections.heading: Priorities and Challenge Areas
sections.body: The source material frames ecosystem development around structure, growth, cyber and cognitive security, information flows, and organizations facing problems that may be addressed by Active Inference.

sections.heading: Domains of Application
sections.body: The PDF maps Active Inference across biology, neuroscience, mental health, bioregional modeling, category theory, computation, economics, education, philosophy, physics, robotics, legal, social, logistics, scientific method, and DeSci.

sections.heading: Ecosystem Support
sections.body: Ecosystem support includes public communication, learning infrastructure, project visibility, partner pathways, research resources, and shared open source tooling.

cards.title: Biology and Neuroscience
cards.text: Modeling living systems, cognition, behavior, and theoretical neurobiology.

cards.title: Mental Health
cards.text: Belief updating, clinical modeling, and therapeutic-relevant theory.

cards.title: Bioregional Modeling
cards.text: Ecological, agricultural, soil, and geospatial applications.

cards.title: Computation
cards.text: Software implementations, agent models, notations, and knowledge infrastructure.

cards.title: Social Systems
cards.text: Group cognition, institutions, coordination, and social science.

cards.title: DeSci
cards.text: Decentralized science, epistemic commons, and open collaboration structures.

pages.title: Get Involved
pages.subtitle: Join the public channels, participate in activities, contribute to projects, or support the Institute.
pages.lede: All backgrounds, time zones, and familiarity levels are welcome. Start with public channels, attend activities, and choose a contribution pathway that fits your context.

sections.heading: Join the Conversation
sections.body: Discord is the main public chat surface identified in the PDF. You can also follow public channels on YouTube, X, Bluesky, Podbean, Facebook, and LinkedIn.

sections.heading: Attend Activities
sections.body: The source highlights weekly and project-specific activities, including textbook groups, learning sessions, model streams, and project meetings. Drop in when your schedule allows.

sections.heading: Choose a Pathway
sections.body: Volunteer, internship, mentorship, fellowship, partnership, philanthropy, grants, open source, and project proposal pathways provide progressively structured ways to participate.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: For questions about activities or the Institute, use the public contact email: blanket@activeinference.institute.

cards.title: Start with Discord
cards.text: Join text and voice conversations with the community.

cards.title: Watch and listen
cards.text: Use the public video and podcast archive to orient yourself.

cards.title: Join an activity
cards.text: Attend a learning group or project meeting when possible.

cards.title: Propose or support a project
cards.text: Use preparation and measurement workflows to make work visible.
